page.title: Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension: 183 alterations and 421 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

literature.title: Efficacy of iron replacement in pulmonary hypertension: A systematic review
literature.authors: Baral T, Malakapogu P, Shyma Z et al.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Without treatment, median survival from diagnosis 2.8 years (pre-modern-therapy era); with current therapy 5-year survival ~50-60%; spontaneous remission essentially unknown
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Triple upfront combination therapy (ambrisentan + tadalafil + selexipag): 41% reduction in clinical failure events (TRITON trial); macitentan + tadalafil initial combination: 41% risk reduction (AMBITION); lung transplantation: 50-60% 5-year survival
remission.gap_size: Large — most patients still receive sequential monotherapy or dual therapy rather than upfront triple therapy
remission.barrier_detail: Upfront combination therapy evidence is robust but drug costs (selexipag alone ~$150K/year) limit access; PAH is often missed or delayed in diagnosis (mean 2-year diagnostic delay); most patients are not treated at PAH expert centres where combination protocols are standard
remission.last_soc_change: 2024 — sotatercept (Winrevair, activin signalling inhibitor) FDA approved — first new mechanism in 15 years; 2017 selexipag approved; upfront dual combination established 2015 (AMBITION); prostacyclin pathway drugs since 1995 (epoprostenol)
remission.outdated_treatments: Sequential monotherapy add-on strategy (evidence strongly favours upfront combination, yet sequential approach remains common); calcium channel blockers without vasoreactivity testing (benefit only in the ~10% who are vasoreactive on right heart catheterisation; often prescribed empirically)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Supervised exercise rehabilitation (once contraindicated; now evidence-based, EFORT trial positive); sotatercept (now approved, anti-activin); iron supplementation (iron deficiency common in PAH, supplementation improves exercise tolerance); potassium channel openers
remission.frontier_signal: Sotatercept (first disease-modifying candidate — reverses vascular remodelling, not just vasodilation); ralinepag (IP receptor agonist, sustained-release); rodatristat ethyl (tryptophan hydroxylase inhibitor for serotonin pathway); HIF inhibitors; cell therapy
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
